projectName: Stock Prediction Platform — Production E2E & Full-Stack Validation
description: Make every Playwright test production-ready against real running services. Remove all fixture mocks from main app tests. Deepen infra tests to assert actual data (not just connectivity). Verify full S&P 500 dashboard, real model artifacts in MinIO, real Kubeflow pipeline runs, all K8s namespaces/pods/jobs visible, Grafana panels with live metrics, Kafka consumer lag tracked, drift events real.

userStories.title: Create production Playwright config and runner script
userStories.description:
Create `services/frontend/playwright.production.config.ts` that:
- Sets baseURL: http://localhost:3000 (frontend port-forward or npm run dev)
- Sets fullyParallel: false, workers: 1 (serial — single Vite/nginx instance)
- Sets defaultTimeout: 30000, navigationTimeout: 45000
- No webServer block (stack already running via port-forwards)
- Includes both main app tests (e2e/*.spec.ts) and infra tests (e2e/infra/*.spec.ts)
- Sets testIdAttribute: 'data-testid'

Create `scripts/run-production-e2e.sh` that:
1. Checks minikube is running (minikube status)
2. Sources existing port-forward logic from deploy-all.sh (or re-runs port-forward section)
3. Waits for all 8 services to be reachable (curl health checks with retry loop: frontend :3000, API :8000, Grafana :3000 mapped to :3001, Prometheus :9090, MinIO console :9001, Kubeflow :8888, K8s dashboard :8443)
4. Exports env vars: GRAFANA_URL, PROMETHEUS_URL, MINIO_URL, KUBEFLOW_URL, K8S_DASHBOARD_URL
5. Prints KUBERNETES_DASHBOARD_TOKEN instructions if not set
6. Runs: cd services/frontend && npx playwright test --config=playwright.production.config.ts
7. Opens HTML report on completion

Also create `services/frontend/e2e/helpers/production-guard.ts` with:
- `requireProductionData(page, checks: Array<{url, description}>)`: calls each URL, throws descriptive error if any returns non-200, otherwise returns true
- `skipIfNotProduction(test, checks)`: wraps requireProductionData in a beforeAll; calls test.skip() with helpful message if checks fail
- Export a standard `PRODUCTION_CHECKS` array covering: health, market/overview (must have >=20 items), predict/bulk?horizon=7, models/comparison (must have >=1 model), models/drift

userStories.title: Dashboard spec — production mode with real S&P 500 data
userStories.description:
Rewrite `services/frontend/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts` for production mode:

**Remove all `page.route()` mock stubs** — let requests hit the real API.

beforeAll guard (use production-guard.ts helper):
- Skip if /health not 200
- Skip if /market/overview returns <20 stocks

Test cases:
1. **Treemap renders ≥20 tickers**: After page load, count visible treemap cells. Assert count >= 20. (selector: look for the treemap cells, which contain ticker text like AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL etc.)
2. **Clicking a real ticker opens detail view**: Click the AAPL cell in treemap. Assert detail section appears with metric cards showing Price, Change, Volume, Market Cap values (not '-' or '--').
3. **Technical indicators toggle works with real data**: In detail view, click 'Show Technical Indicators'. Assert the TA panel appears. Assert at least one indicator value is visible (RSI should show a number between 0-100).
4. **Navigation to all 4 app pages works**: Click each sidebar link (Forecasts, Models, Drift, Backtest). Assert URL changes and page heading is correct.
5. **Close detail view**: Open detail, click X/Close, assert detail section hidden.

Keep `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })` on all describe blocks.
Increase timeout to 20000ms per test (real API calls are slower than mocks).

userStories.title: Forecasts spec — production mode with real predictions
userStories.description:
Rewrite `services/frontend/e2e/forecasts.spec.ts` for production mode:

**Remove all `page.route()` mock stubs.**

beforeAll guard:
- Skip if /health not 200
- Skip if /predict/bulk?horizon=7 returns <10 predictions
- Skip if /market/overview returns <10 stocks

Test cases:
1. **Table shows ≥10 stocks**: After page load, count table rows. Assert >= 10.
2. **Model name shown (not fixture_ prefix)**: Assert the model name banner does NOT start with 'fixture_'. It should show a real model name.
3. **Horizon toggle 7D → 30D refetches**: Click 30D button. Assert table reloads (loading spinner, then rows). Assert row count still >= 10.
4. **Search filters table**: Type 'AAPL' in search input. Assert only AAPL row(s) visible. Clear search. Assert full table returns.
5. **Clicking row opens detail view**: Click AAPL row. Assert '{ticker} — Detail View' heading appears. Assert chart container visible. Assert SHAP panel or indicators visible.
6. **Export buttons enabled**: Assert CSV and PDF export buttons are not disabled when data is loaded.

keep serial mode. Set timeout 25000ms.

userStories.title: Models spec — production mode with real trained model data
userStories.description:
Rewrite `services/frontend/e2e/models.spec.ts` for production mode:

**Remove all `page.route()` mock stubs.**

beforeAll guard:
- Skip if /health not 200
- Skip if /models/comparison returns 0 models

Test cases:
1. **Winner card shows real model**: Assert WinnerCard renders. Assert model name does NOT contain 'fixture_'. Assert at least one metric (RMSE, MAE, R²) shows a real number (not '-').
2. **Model table has ≥1 row**: Assert table has at least 1 row of model data.
3. **Winner is auto-selected, SHAP panel loads**: The winner model row should be pre-selected. Assert SHAP Feature Importance section is visible in the detail panel. Assert at least 3 feature names are shown.
4. **Clicking a different model updates detail panel**: If table has ≥2 models, click the second model row. Assert detail panel updates (model name in panel changes).
5. **Search filters by real model name**: Take the winner model name from the WinnerCard. Type first 4 chars into search. Assert table is filtered to ≤3 results.
6. **Export buttons enabled**: Assert CSV + PDF buttons are not disabled.

Serial mode. Timeout 25000ms.

userStories.title: Drift spec — production mode with real drift monitoring data
userStories.description:
Rewrite `services/frontend/e2e/drift.spec.ts` for production mode:

**Remove all `page.route()` mock stubs.**

beforeAll guard:
- Skip if /health not 200
- Skip if /models/comparison returns 0 models (ActiveModelCard needs this)
- Skip if /models/drift is not 200

Test cases:
1. **Heading renders**: Assert 'Drift Monitoring' heading visible.
2. **ActiveModelCard shows real model with 'Active' badge**: Assert model name shown does NOT start with 'fixture_'. Assert badge with text 'Active' visible.
3. **RollingPerformanceChart container visible**: Assert the rolling performance chart container is in the DOM and has non-zero height. (No assertion on data points — chart may be empty if no recent predictions.)
4. **DriftTimeline renders**: Assert the drift timeline section renders. If drift events exist, assert at least one event row is visible. If no events, assert empty state message is shown — either is acceptable.
5. **RetrainStatusPanel renders**: Assert retrain status panel is visible with some content (previous model or 'No previous model' message).
6. **No unhandled loading state**: After 10 seconds, assert no loading spinners remain visible.

Serial mode. Timeout 30000ms.

userStories.title: Backtest spec — production mode with real historical data
userStories.description:
Rewrite `services/frontend/e2e/backtest.spec.ts` for production mode:

**Remove all `page.route()` mock stubs.**

beforeAll guard:
- Skip if /health not 200
- Skip if /market/overview returns <5 stocks

Test cases:
1. **Page loads and shows model info**: Assert model info banner visible. Model name must NOT start with 'fixture_'. Assert horizon shown (7d or similar).
2. **Ticker select populated with real tickers**: Open ticker dropdown. Assert ≥5 ticker options visible. AAPL should be among them.
3. **Date inputs and horizon select present**: Assert start date input, end date input, and horizon select are present and interactable.
4. **Run Backtest with AAPL produces metrics**: Select AAPL. Set start date to 1 year ago (calculate dynamically: new Date(); setFullYear(year-1); format YYYY-MM-DD). Set end date to today. Click 'Run Backtest'. Wait up to 20s for results. Assert BacktestMetricsSummary shows RMSE > 0 and MAE > 0.
5. **Chart renders after backtest run**: Assert backtest chart container is visible and has non-zero height.
6. **Export buttons enabled after run**: Assert CSV + PDF export buttons not disabled.

Serial mode. Timeout 30000ms.

userStories.title: Grafana spec — verify panels contain real metric data
userStories.description:
Expand `services/frontend/e2e/infra/grafana.spec.ts` with deeper data assertions:

Keep existing probe, login, and datasource tests.

**Add new test suite: 'Dashboard panel data'** (requires login):

For each of the 3 dashboards (API Health, ML Performance, Kafka & Infrastructure), after navigating to the dashboard:
1. **Wait for panels to finish loading**: Use `page.waitForSelector('.panel-container:not(.panel-container--transparent)', { timeout: 20000 })`. Assert at least 1 non-transparent panel rendered.
2. **Assert no 'No data' panels for key metrics**:
   - API Health dashboard: Find a panel matching 'Request Rate' or 'Error Rate'. Assert it does NOT show 'No data' text. (Selector: look for `.panel-content` that contains 'No data'.)
   - ML Performance dashboard: Same check for 'Predictions' or 'Inference' panels.
   - Kafka dashboard: Same check for 'Consumer Lag' or 'Messages' panels.
3. **Prometheus datasource query test**: Use Grafana HTTP API to run a PromQL query:
   - GET /api/datasources/proxy/1/api/v1/query?query=up
   - Assert response has status 'success' and result array length > 0
   - Assert at least one target with job='kubernetes-pods' is in the 'up' results

**Add new test suite: 'Alert rules configured'**:
- GET /api/ruler/grafana/api/v1/rules (Grafana managed alerts API)
- Assert response contains at least 1 rule group with rules
- Assert rule names include at least one of: HighDriftSeverity, HighAPIErrorRate, HighConsumerLag

Keep service probe at top. All new suites skip gracefully if API calls return unexpected status.

userStories.title: K8s Dashboard spec — verify all namespaces, pods, and CronJobs
userStories.description:
Expand `services/frontend/e2e/infra/k8s-dashboard.spec.ts` with full workload verification:

Keep existing two-stage skip (token check + service probe) and auth test.

**Add new test suite: 'Namespaces'** (timeout 60s):
1. Navigate to Namespaces section (sidebar link or /#!/namespace if hash-routing).
2. Wait for namespace list to render.
3. Assert each of these namespace names is visible in the list: storage, ingestion, processing, ml, frontend, monitoring.
4. (kubeflow and kubernetes-dashboard are optional — assert they appear if KFP is installed.)

**Add new test suite: 'Pods are running'** (timeout 90s):
1. Navigate to Pods section (all namespaces view).
2. Wait for pod list to render.
3. Assert at least 8 pods are shown in the list.
4. Assert no pod rows show 'CrashLoopBackOff' or 'Error' status.
5. Assert at least 1 pod in namespace 'storage' (PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB).
6. Assert at least 1 pod in namespace 'ingestion' (FastAPI).
7. Assert at least 1 pod in namespace 'monitoring' (Prometheus or Grafana).

**Add new test suite: 'CronJobs'** (timeout 60s):
1. Navigate to CronJobs section.
2. Assert at least 3 CronJobs visible (intraday-ingestion, historical-ingestion, ml-training-job or similar names).
3. For each CronJob, assert status is not 'Failed'.

**Add new test suite: 'Services'** (timeout 60s):
1. Navigate to Services section.
2. Assert at least 5 services are listed.
3. Assert 'postgres' or 'postgresql' service appears in storage namespace.

Note: K8s Dashboard uses hash routing (#/!/ paths). Use DOM-based navigation (click sidebar links) not waitForURL. All new suites inherit the top-level skip logic.

userStories.title: MinIO spec — verify model artifacts and drift logs exist in buckets
userStories.description:
Expand `services/frontend/e2e/infra/minio.spec.ts` to verify actual objects exist:

Keep existing probe, login, and bucket existence tests.

**Add new test suite: 'model-artifacts bucket has content'** (timeout 45s):
1. After login, navigate to model-artifacts bucket (click bucket row by text).
2. Assert object browser renders (URL contains 'browser/model-artifacts' or similar).
3. Wait for object list to load.
4. Assert at least 1 object or folder is visible in the bucket. (Look for file/folder rows in the object table.)
5. Assert that a file matching 'pipeline.pkl' OR a folder named after a model version is visible. (This confirms model training has run and artifacts were uploaded.)

**Add new test suite: 'drift-logs bucket has content'** (timeout 45s):
1. Navigate back to bucket list (breadcrumb or back button).
2. Click drift-logs bucket.
3. Assert at least 1 file is visible in the bucket.
4. Assert a file with extension '.json' or '.jsonl' is visible. (Confirms drift monitoring has been logging.)

**Add new test suite: 'MinIO metrics endpoint reachable'** (no UI, API call):
1. page.request.get(MINIO_URL.replace(':9001', ':9000') + '/minio/health/live')
2. Assert status is 200.
3. page.request.get(MINIO_URL.replace(':9001', ':9000') + '/minio/health/ready')
4. Assert status is 200.

Note: If model-artifacts is empty (training never ran), this test should NOT fail the suite — wrap the content assertions in a conditional: if bucket appears empty, log a warning via console.warn and call test.fixme('model training has not run yet — no artifacts in MinIO') to mark it as fixme rather than failing.

userStories.title: Kubeflow spec — verify pipeline registered and at least one run completed
userStories.description:
Expand `services/frontend/e2e/infra/kubeflow.spec.ts` with pipeline content verification:

Keep existing probe and basic page render tests.

**Add new test suite: 'Pipeline registered'** (timeout 60s):
1. Navigate to Pipelines list (hash routing: /#/pipelines).
2. Wait for pipeline list to load (look for table rows or empty state).
3. If pipeline table has 0 rows:
   a. Log instructions: 'Run: kubectl apply -f k8s/ml/kubeflow/kfp-compile-job.yaml -n ml'
   b. Call test.fixme('KFP pipeline not yet compiled — run the compile job first')
4. If pipeline table has ≥1 row:
   a. Assert a row containing 'stock' or 'training' or 'prediction' in the pipeline name.
   b. Click the pipeline row to open pipeline graph view.
   c. Assert pipeline graph SVG or node list renders.
   d. Assert at least 5 pipeline steps/nodes visible (data_loading, feature_engineering, label_generation, train_models, evaluation, model_comparison, winner_selection, model_persistence — at least 5 of these).

**Add new test suite: 'Pipeline run history'** (timeout 60s):
1. Navigate to Runs page (hash routing: /#/runs).
2. Wait for runs list to load.
3. If no runs:
   a. test.fixme('No KFP pipeline runs found — trigger a run via the KFP UI or the submit-pipeline.sh script')
4. If ≥1 run:
   a. Assert at least 1 run row visible.
   b. Assert the most recent run has status: 'Succeeded' or 'Running'. (Avoid asserting 'Failed'.)
   c. Click the most recent Succeeded run if one exists.
   d. Assert run detail page shows step execution graph.
   e. Assert at least 3 steps show 'Succeeded' status chips.

**Add new test suite: 'Experiments'** (timeout 45s):
1. Navigate to Experiments page.
2. Assert page renders without error.
3. If experiments exist, assert at least 1 experiment row.

All suites use graceful test.fixme() rather than test failure when KFP is installed but has no data yet.

userStories.title: Prometheus spec — verify targets are UP and alert rules fire correctly
userStories.description:
Expand `services/frontend/e2e/infra/prometheus.spec.ts` with target health and rule verification:

Keep existing probe, query execution, and targets page tests.

**Add new test suite: 'Critical targets are UP'** (timeout 45s):
1. Navigate to /targets page.
2. Wait for targets list to render.
3. Assert the following scrape jobs are visible and UP:
   - kubernetes-pods (at least 2 endpoints UP)
   - Assert no job shows '0/X up' (all pods in a job unreachable)
4. Assert total UP targets >= 5.

**Add new test suite: 'PromQL sanity queries'** (API-based, no UI):
For each query, use page.request.get(`${PROMETHEUS_URL}/api/v1/query?query=<QUERY>`) and assert result.data.result.length > 0:
1. `up{job="kubernetes-pods"}` — at least 3 pods are UP
2. `http_requests_total` OR `starlette_requests_total` — FastAPI is emitting metrics
3. `kafka_consumer_lag_sum` OR `kafka_consumer_group_lag` — Kafka consumer lag metric exists
4. `process_cpu_seconds_total` — generic process metric (confirms scraping works)

For queries 2 and 3: if result is empty, call test.fixme() with message explaining which K8s service to check — do NOT hard-fail since metric names depend on library versions.

**Add new test suite: 'Alert rules are configured'** (timeout 30s):
1. Navigate to /alerts page.
2. Assert page renders.
3. Use API: GET /api/v1/rules?type=alert
4. Parse response. Assert rules array has >= 3 alert rules.
5. Assert rule names include HighDriftSeverity AND HighAPIErrorRate AND HighConsumerLag.

**Expand existing targets test**:
- After asserting kubernetes-pods scrape job exists, also assert the scrape job for FastAPI pods specifically is present (look for pod annotations — job may be 'kubernetes-pods' with label filtering).

userStories.title: Add production test npm scripts and verify CI compatibility
userStories.description:
Update `services/frontend/package.json` to add production test commands:

```json
"test:e2e": "playwright test --config=playwright.config.ts",
"test:e2e:prod": "playwright test --config=playwright.production.config.ts",
"test:infra": "playwright test --config=playwright.infra.config.ts",
"test:all": "npm run test:e2e && npm run test:infra"
```

Also verify that all 5 main spec files (dashboard, models, forecasts, drift, backtest) have been updated from previous stories and run the production config successfully:

```bash
cd stock-prediction-platform/services/frontend
npx playwright test --config=playwright.production.config.ts --list
```

This should list all tests without errors. Fix any import errors, missing helpers, or config issues.

Also ensure the original fixture-based tests still work (for CI use without a running cluster):
```bash
npx playwright test --config=playwright.config.ts --list
```
Both configs should list tests without TypeScript errors.

Run a quick smoke test of the fixture-based suite to confirm nothing was accidentally broken by the production mode changes:
```bash
npx playwright test --config=playwright.config.ts --dry-run
```

Commit all changes from E2E-001 through E2E-012 if not already committed per-story.

userStories.title: Create comprehensive port-forward health-check and test orchestration script
userStories.description:
Create `stock-prediction-platform/scripts/test-production.sh`:

This script is the single entry point to run all production E2E tests.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Usage: ./scripts/test-production.sh [--skip-infra] [--skip-app] [--report]
set -e
```

**Step 1 — Preflight checks**:
- Verify minikube running: `minikube status | grep -q 'host: Running'`
- Verify kubectl available and context is minikube
- Verify jq, node, npx available
- Print cluster info summary: nodes, namespaces

**Step 2 — Port-forward management**:
- Kill any stale port-forwards from previous runs (kill PIDs in /tmp/pf-*.pid)
- Start port-forwards (re-use the logic from deploy-all.sh port-forward section):
  - Frontend: kubectl port-forward -n frontend svc/frontend-service 3000:80
  - API: kubectl port-forward -n ingestion svc/stock-api-service 8000:8000
  - Grafana: kubectl port-forward -n monitoring svc/grafana 3001:3000
  - Prometheus: kubectl port-forward -n monitoring svc/prometheus 9090:9090
  - MinIO console: kubectl port-forward -n storage svc/minio 9001:9001
  - MinIO API: kubectl port-forward -n storage svc/minio 9000:9000
  - Kubeflow: kubectl port-forward -n kubeflow svc/ml-pipeline-ui 8888:80 2>/dev/null || echo 'KFP not installed, skipping'
  - K8s Dashboard: kubectl port-forward -n kubernetes-dashboard svc/kubernetes-dashboard 8443:443 2>/dev/null || echo 'K8s dashboard not installed, skipping'
- Save PIDs to /tmp/pf-*.pid
- Wait for all services to become reachable (curl retry loop, max 60s each, print progress)

**Step 3 — Environment exports**:
```bash
export GRAFANA_URL=http://localhost:3001
export PROMETHEUS_URL=http://localhost:9090
export MINIO_URL=http://localhost:9001
export KUBEFLOW_URL=http://localhost:8888
export K8S_DASHBOARD_URL=http://localhost:8443
export BASE_API_URL=http://localhost:8000
```
Print KUBERNETES_DASHBOARD_TOKEN instructions if not set.

**Step 4 — Run tests**:
```bash
cd stock-prediction-platform/services/frontend
# Run app tests (production mode)
npx playwright test --config=playwright.production.config.ts $APP_ARGS
# Run infra tests
npx playwright test --config=playwright.infra.config.ts $INFRA_ARGS
```

**Step 5 — Cleanup**:
- Kill port-forward PIDs
- Print summary: tests passed / failed, link to HTML report

Make executable. Test it runs without errors (even if some tests skip due to services being down).

userStories.title: Verify all backend API endpoints return production data for S&P 500
userStories.description:
Create `services/frontend/e2e/api-health.spec.ts` — API-only tests (no UI) that verify backend data quality:

Use playwright's APIRequestContext (no page/browser needed, just request). Set baseURL to process.env.BASE_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000'.

Test suite: 'Backend API data quality' (skip if /health not 200)

1. **GET /health** → status 200, body has {status: 'ok' or similar}
2. **GET /market/overview** → status 200, array length >= 20, each item has ticker, price, change_pct, market_cap fields, no null prices
3. **GET /predict/bulk?horizon=7** → status 200, array length >= 10, each item has ticker, predicted_price, model_name fields, model_name does NOT start with 'fixture_'
4. **GET /predict/AAPL?horizon=7** → status 200, response has predicted_price > 0, confidence_metrics present
5. **GET /models/comparison** → status 200, array length >= 1, winner model has is_winner: true, metrics has rmse and mae as numbers
6. **GET /models/drift** → status 200, response has drift_events array (may be empty) and active_model object with model_name that doesn't start with 'fixture_'
7. **GET /market/indicators/AAPL** → status 200, response has rsi, macd_line, sma_20 as numbers
8. **GET /predict/bulk?horizon=30** → status 200, array length >= 10 (30-day horizon also works)

For any endpoint that returns unexpected data, print the actual response body in the test error message to aid debugging.

Add to playwright.production.config.ts test file list: './e2e/api-health.spec.ts'.

This spec has NO `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })` — these are independent stateless checks and can run in parallel.

userStories.title: Ensure Grafana dashboards have correct panel definitions and Kafka metrics tracked
userStories.description:
Audit and fix the existing Grafana dashboard ConfigMaps in `k8s/monitoring/` to ensure:

1. **API Health dashboard** (`k8s/monitoring/grafana-dashboard-api.yaml` or similar) includes panels for:
   - Request Rate (PromQL: `rate(starlette_requests_total[5m])` or `rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count[5m])`)
   - Error Rate % (PromQL: `rate(starlette_requests_total{status_code=~"5.."}[5m]) / rate(starlette_requests_total[5m]) * 100`)
   - Response Latency P95 (PromQL: `histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(starlette_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m]))`)
   - Active pods (PromQL: `count(up{namespace="ingestion"})`)

2. **ML Performance dashboard** includes panels for:
   - Predictions by Model (PromQL: `sum by (model_name) (predictions_generated_total)` — use whatever metric name the FastAPI exposes)
   - Inference Errors (PromQL: `rate(prediction_errors_total[5m])`)
   - Last Training Time (PromQL: `model_last_trained_timestamp`)
   - Drift Severity (PromQL: `drift_severity_score` or `drift_events_total`)

3. **Kafka & Infrastructure dashboard** includes panels for:
   - Consumer Lag (PromQL: `kafka_consumer_lag_sum` or `kafka_consumergroup_lag`)
   - Messages Consumed Rate (PromQL: `rate(kafka_consumer_records_consumed_total[5m])`)
   - Pod CPU usage (PromQL: `rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{namespace=~"ingestion|processing|ml"}[5m])`)
   - Pod Memory usage (PromQL: `container_memory_working_set_bytes{namespace=~"ingestion|processing|ml"}`)

For each dashboard YAML that is missing panels, ADD the missing panels to the ConfigMap JSON.
Also check `monitoring/prometheus.yml` — ensure it has scrape configs for:
- FastAPI pods (should already be there via kubernetes-pods job)
- Kafka JMX exporter or Strimzi kafka-exporter (add if missing)

After updating YAMLs, provide the kubectl apply command to reload ConfigMaps:
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/monitoring/grafana-dashboards-configmap.yaml -n monitoring
kubectl rollout restart deployment/grafana -n monitoring
```

Note: Do not change panel titles — the Playwright tests assert on the existing titles ('Request Rate', 'Error Rate %', 'Predictions by Model', 'Inference Errors', 'Messages Consumed Rate', 'Consumer Lag').

userStories.title: Add K8s Dashboard and Kubeflow deployment manifests if missing
userStories.description:
Check and ensure K8s Dashboard and Kubeflow are deployable via deploy-all.sh:

**K8s Dashboard:**
1. Check if `k8s/` has a kubernetes-dashboard manifest. If not, create `k8s/dashboard/kubernetes-dashboard.yaml` using the recommended install manifest URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/dashboard/v2.7.0/aio/deploy/recommended.yaml (download and commit the content — do not use remote URL at deploy time).
2. Ensure deploy-all.sh has a section that applies the K8s dashboard manifest and creates a service account + cluster role binding for token-based access.
3. Add a helper in deploy-all.sh:
```bash
echo '=== K8s Dashboard Token ==='
kubectl create serviceaccount dashboard-admin -n kubernetes-dashboard --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
kubectl create clusterrolebinding dashboard-admin --clusterrole=cluster-admin --serviceaccount=kubernetes-dashboard:dashboard-admin --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
KUBERNETES_DASHBOARD_TOKEN=$(kubectl create token dashboard-admin -n kubernetes-dashboard)
echo "KUBERNETES_DASHBOARD_TOKEN=$KUBERNETES_DASHBOARD_TOKEN"
echo "Export with: export KUBERNETES_DASHBOARD_TOKEN='$KUBERNETES_DASHBOARD_TOKEN'"
```

**Kubeflow Pipelines:**
1. Check if deploy-all.sh already installs KFP (it does — Phase 20 section).
2. Verify `k8s/ml/kubeflow/kfp-compile-job.yaml` exists. If not, create it — a K8s Job that runs `python ml/pipelines/training_pipeline.py --compile-only` in the ml-pipeline Docker image, outputs compiled pipeline YAML to MinIO.
3. Ensure the KFP pipeline registration step in deploy-all.sh includes submitting the compiled pipeline to KFP via the Python SDK or REST API after compilation.
4. Add a `scripts/submit-pipeline.sh` if it doesn't exist:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Submit the compiled stock prediction pipeline to KFP
KFP_URL=${KFP_URL:-http://localhost:8888}
python3 -c "
import kfp
client = kfp.Client(host='$KFP_URL')
client.upload_pipeline('ml/pipelines/compiled_pipeline.yaml', pipeline_name='stock-prediction-training')
print('Pipeline uploaded successfully')
"
```

Commit all new/modified files.

userStories.title: Migrate frontend to Material UI (MUI) v5 — install, theme, and replace Tailwind primitives
userStories.description:
Install MUI v5 and set up a Bloomberg Terminal-inspired dark theme. This story covers foundation only — install + theme + layout shell.

**Install:**
```bash
cd stock-prediction-platform/services/frontend
npm install @mui/material @mui/icons-material @emotion/react @emotion/styled @mui/x-data-grid @mui/x-charts
```

**Create `src/theme/index.ts`** — MUI dark theme:
```ts
import { createTheme } from '@mui/material/styles';
export const theme = createTheme({
  palette: {
    mode: 'dark',
    primary: { main: '#00bcd4' },       // Bloomberg cyan
    secondary: { main: '#ff9800' },     // amber accent
    success: { main: '#4caf50' },
    error: { main: '#f44336' },
    background: { default: '#0a0e1a', paper: '#111827' },
    text: { primary: '#e8eaf6', secondary: '#9fa8da' },
  },
  typography: {
    fontFamily: '"IBM Plex Mono", "Roboto Mono", monospace',
    h4: { fontWeight: 700, letterSpacing: '0.05em' },
    h6: { fontWeight: 600 },
    body2: { fontSize: '0.8rem' },
  },
  components: {
    MuiPaper: { styleOverrides: { root: { backgroundImage: 'none', border: '1px solid rgba(0,188,212,0.12)' } } },
    MuiTableCell: { styleOverrides: { head: { backgroundColor: '#0d1117', color: '#9fa8da', fontWeight: 700 } } },
    MuiChip: { styleOverrides: { root: { borderRadius: 4 } } },
  }
});
```

**Wrap app in `<ThemeProvider theme={theme}><CssBaseline />`** in `src/main.tsx`.

**Replace Layout/Sidebar** with MUI components:
- Use `<Drawer variant="permanent">` for sidebar (width 220px)
- Use `<AppBar position="fixed">` for top bar showing 'S&P 500 AI Platform' + connection status chip
- Use `<Box component="main" sx={{ ml: '220px' }}>` for content area
- Sidebar items use `<ListItemButton>` with `<ListItemIcon>` (use MUI icons: DashboardIcon, TrendingUpIcon, AccountTreeIcon, BubbleChartIcon, SsidChartIcon)
- Active page highlighted with primary color border-left

**Run build to verify no TypeScript errors:**
```bash
npm run build
```

Commit with: `feat(UI-001): install MUI v5 dark theme and replace layout shell`

userStories.title: Replace all page components with MUI — Dashboard, Forecasts, Models
userStories.description:
Upgrade the three data-heavy pages to use MUI components fully. Keep existing logic/hooks — only replace presentational elements.

**Dashboard page (`src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` + components/dashboard/):**
- Wrap page in `<Container maxWidth="xl">`
- Metric cards → `<Card>` with `<CardContent>`, `<Typography variant="h4">` for values, colored with `sx={{ color: change >= 0 ? 'success.main' : 'error.main' }}`
- Add `<Grid container spacing={2}>` for top metric cards row (Price, Change%, Volume, Market Cap)
- Treemap container → `<Paper sx={{ p: 2, height: 400 }}>` wrapper
- Technical indicators panel → `<Accordion>` component (expandable)
- Stock detail section → `<Drawer anchor="right" open={!!selectedTicker}>` (slide-in panel, 480px wide)
- Add a `<Skeleton variant="rectangular" height={400} />` loading state for treemap

**Forecasts page (`src/pages/Forecasts.tsx` + components/forecasts/):**
- Replace custom table with `<DataGrid>` from @mui/x-data-grid:
  - Columns: Ticker, Current Price, Predicted Price, Expected Return%, Confidence, Trend chip, Actions
  - Trend chip: `<Chip label="Bullish" color="success" size="small" />` etc.
  - Built-in sorting, filtering, pagination (pageSize: 25)
  - columnVisibilityModel for responsive hiding
- Horizon toggle → `<ToggleButtonGroup exclusive>` with `<ToggleButton>` for 1D, 7D, 30D
- Search → `<TextField size="small" InputProps={{ startAdornment: <SearchIcon/> }}>`
- Stock detail drawer → same `<Drawer anchor="right">` pattern as Dashboard
- Export buttons → `<ButtonGroup variant="outlined" size="small">`

**Models page (`src/pages/Models.tsx` + components/tables/):**
- Winner card → `<Card sx={{ border: '2px solid', borderColor: 'primary.main' }}>` with `<Chip label="Winner" color="primary" />` in top-right
- Model comparison table → `<DataGrid>` with columns: Model Name, RMSE, MAE, MAPE, R², Dir.Accuracy, Folds Stability
- Winning row highlighted with `getRowClassName` returning a CSS class with primary color background
- SHAP panel → `<Paper>` with horizontal bar chart using `@mui/x-charts <BarChart layout="horizontal">`
- Model metrics → two-column `<Grid>` with in-sample vs out-of-sample metrics side by side using `<Divider orientation="vertical" flexItem>`

**Run build + lint after each page. Commit per page.**

userStories.title: Replace Drift and Backtest pages with MUI — add real-time status indicators
userStories.description:
Complete the MUI migration for remaining pages and add production-ready status indicators.

**Drift page (`src/pages/Drift.tsx` + components/drift/):**
- ActiveModelCard → `<Card>` with colored status strip on left border (green=active, orange=stale)
  - Model name in `<Typography variant="h6">`
  - Metrics in `<Stack direction="row" spacing={3}>` with label/value pairs
  - `<Chip label="Active" color="success" />` or `<Chip label="Retraining..." color="warning" icon={<CircularProgress size={12}/>}/>`
- DriftTimeline → `<Timeline>` from `@mui/lab` (install: `npm install @mui/lab`):
  - Each drift event as `<TimelineItem>` with `<TimelineDot color={severity === 'high' ? 'error' : 'warning'}>` 
  - Event description in `<TimelineContent><Paper>` with type badge and feature list
- RollingPerformanceChart → wrap in `<Paper sx={{ p: 2 }}>` with `<Typography variant="subtitle2">` title
- RetrainStatusPanel → `<Card>` with two columns: Previous Model vs Current Model, separated by `<Divider>`
- FeatureDistributionChart → `<Accordion>` (collapsed by default to save space)
- Add a `<Alert severity="warning">` banner at top if any drift event has severity 'high' in the last 7 days

**Backtest page (`src/pages/Backtest.tsx` + components/backtest/):**
- Control panel → `<Paper sx={{ p: 3 }}>` with `<Grid container spacing={2}>`:
  - Ticker: `<Autocomplete options={tickers} renderInput={(params) => <TextField {...params} label="Ticker" />}`
  - Start Date / End Date: `<TextField type="date" InputLabelProps={{ shrink: true }}>`
  - Horizon: `<Select>` with `<MenuItem>` options
  - Run button: `<LoadingButton loading={isRunning} variant="contained" startIcon={<PlayArrowIcon/>}>`  (install @mui/lab LoadingButton)
- Metrics summary → `<Grid container spacing={2}>` with 4 `<Card>` metric tiles:
  - RMSE (red if > threshold), MAE (red if > threshold), Directional Accuracy (green if > 60%), R²
  - Each tile: big number in `<Typography variant="h3">`, label below in `<Typography variant="caption">`
- Backtest chart → wrap in `<Paper sx={{ p: 2, height: 350 }}>` 
- Export → `<ButtonGroup>` same as forecasts

**Global polish:**
- Add `<Tooltip title="...">` to all icon buttons and metric values
- Add `<LinearProgress>` at top of page during data loading (position: fixed, top: 0)
- Ensure all pages have consistent `<PageHeader>` using `<Typography variant="h4" sx={{ mb: 3 }}>` 
- Remove all remaining Tailwind class names from modified components (check with grep 'className="' to find any leftover)

**Run:** `npm run build && grep -r 'className="[a-z]' src/pages/ src/components/` — should return 0 results for Tailwind classes.

Commit: `feat(UI-003): complete MUI migration — Drift + Backtest + global polish`

userStories.title: Add real-time connection status bar, live price ticker, and K8s health panel
userStories.description: Add production-facing status features that show the platform is live. **1. Connection status bar in AppBar:** In the top AppBar, add a right-aligned `` with: - API status chip: `} label="API" color={apiUp ? 'success' : 'error'} variant="outlined">` — polls /health every 30s - Kafka status chip: same pattern — inferred from /models/drift response (if stale > 1h, show warning) - DB status chip: inferred from /health response fields - Last update timestamp: `Updated: HH:MM:SS` **2. Live price ticker strip (top of Dashboard page):** Add a horizontally scrolling ticker strip showing live prices for top 10 S&P 500 stocks: - Fetch /market/overview, take first 10 stocks sorted by market_cap - Display as scrolling `` replacement using CSS animation (no tag): ```tsx {stocks.map(s => ...)} ``` - Each item: `AAPL $182.40 +1.2%` - Add CSS keyframe in index.css: `@keyframes scroll-left { from { transform: translateX(100%); } to { transform: translateX(-100%); } }` - Auto-refreshes every 60s **3. K8s health mini-panel in sidebar footer:** At the bottom of the sidebar Drawer, add a collapsed section showing: - Fetch /health endpoint — if it has k8s_info or pod_info fields, display them - If not, add a new `/health/k8s` endpoint to FastAPI (services/api/app/routers/health.py) that runs: ```python import subprocess result = subprocess.run(['kubectl', 'get', 'pods', '-A', '--field-selector=status.phase=Running', '-o', 'json'], capture_output=True, timeout=5) ``` Returns: `{ running_pods: N, namespaces: [...] }` — only if kubectl is available in the API pod (optional feature, graceful fallback) - Display: `Pods: 12 running` with a green dot - If kubectl not available: show nothing (don't error) **4. Update Playwright tests for new elements:** In `e2e/dashboard.spec.ts` (production mode), add: - Assert AppBar connection status chips are visible (look for role='status' or chip with text 'API') - Assert ticker strip is visible at top of dashboard page Commit: `feat(UI-004): add live connection status bar, price ticker strip, and K8s health panel`

userStories.title: Final validation — run full production test suite and fix all failures
userStories.description:
This is the integration story. Run the full production test suite and fix any remaining failures:

```bash
cd /path/to/repo
bash scripts/test-production.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-output.txt
```

For EACH failing test:
1. Read the error message carefully
2. Identify root cause (missing data, wrong selector, timing issue, config problem)
3. Fix the root cause (update the spec, fix backend config, update K8s manifest)
4. Re-run just that spec file to confirm fix
5. Commit the fix

Tests that use `test.fixme()` are acceptable — those indicate infrastructure gaps (KFP not yet run, no drift events yet) and are not failures.
Tests that use `test.skip()` due to service being unreachable are acceptable if the service isn't deployed.

Target outcome:
- 0 hard failures (FAIL)
- Some fixme/skip is OK for services not yet bootstrapped (KFP runs, MinIO artifacts)
- All 5 main app specs: PASS (no fixture mocks, real data)
- grafana.spec.ts: PASS (panels visible, datasources configured, alert rules present)
- prometheus.spec.ts: PASS (targets UP, PromQL works, alert rules listed)
- minio.spec.ts: PASS (login, bucket existence — content may be fixme if empty)
- kubeflow.spec.ts: PASS or FIXME (UI renders; pipeline/run may be fixme if not bootstrapped)
- k8s-dashboard.spec.ts: PASS or SKIP (if token not available)
- api-health.spec.ts: PASS (all endpoints return production data)

After all fixes are applied and committed, update scripts/ralph/prd.json to mark all stories passes: true and append a final summary to scripts/ralph/progress.txt.

Then emit: <promise>COMPLETE</promise>
